title: The SE Challenge: Railway Signaling Subsystem
description: Apply everything you have learned to design a railway signaling subsystem.

phases.title: Phase 1: Stakeholders & Requirements
phases.scenario: You are the lead SE for a new CBTC (Communications-Based Train Control) system. Identify stakeholders, define SMART requirements, and build a traceability matrix.

items.text: Select verification methods for safety requirements

phases.learningObjective: Integrate stakeholder analysis and requirements engineering
phases.hint: Think about who influences the project and what measurable outcomes they need.

phases.title: Phase 2: Architecture & Trade-offs
phases.scenario: Decompose the CBTC system into subsystems, define interfaces, and allocate your budget across performance, safety, and maintainability.
phases.learningObjective: Apply architecture design and trade-off analysis
phases.hint: Consider how each analysis method maps to the architectural decision being made.

phases.title: Phase 3: V&V & Compliance
phases.scenario: Plan verification and validation for the CBTC system. Ensure compliance with EN 50126 RAMS requirements.
phases.learningObjective: Plan V&V and ensure RAMS compliance
phases.hint: Think about the V-model lifecycle: hazards must be identified before safety cases can be developed.

phases.title: Phase 4: Integration & Risk Management
phases.scenario: Plan the integration sequence, assess risks, and assemble the SEMP for the CBTC project.
phases.learningObjective: Integrate systems and manage project risks
phases.hint: Consider how integration, risk, configuration, and management processes work together.
